title: IP address following the "--gateway" argument in pasta_options fails to parse
body:
### Issue Description

My ~/.config/containers/containers.conf is set to the following:

```
[network]
pasta_options = ["--gateway", "10.10.0.171"]
```

Trying to run ```podman unshare --rootless-netns /bin/bash``` fails with 
```
Error: rootless netns: cleanup: 1 error occurred:
	* rootless netns: kill network process: strconv.Atoi: parsing "": invalid syntax
```

If I replace ```--gateway``` with ```--dns```, the config is fine. If I instead follow ```--gateway``` with a malformed IP address, parsing fails as expected with "Invalid gateway address".

comments.body:
That's indeed a bug when pasta creates or leaves an empty file, but podman (c/common) assumes there will always be a PID _if_ the file exists, which seems not to be the case.

https://github.com/containers/container-libs/blob/f0ddf1a745bb52c26889dcd69499c2dd9d4f40c1/common/libnetwork/internal/rootlessnetns/netns_linux.go#L650-L656

That being said, it looks like we might be masking an underlying error if the cleanup fails. cc/ @sbrivio-rh

Did you actually make sure the gateway is valid compared to the default subnet pasta picks?

In particular what happens if you pass that one the cli
```
$ podman run --network pasta:--gateway,1.1.1.1 quay.io/libpod/testimage:20241011 true
Error: pasta failed with exit code 1:
Couldn't set IPv4 route(s) in guest: Network is unreachable
```

Trying with `--network pasta:--gateway,1.1.1.1,--pid,/tmp/pasta.pid` shows that it fails to startup and created an empty pid file and thus podman does indeed fail to read it.
I guess we could handle this in podman to ignore empty pid file there.

> Did you actually make sure the gateway is valid compared to the default subnet pasta picks?

No; notwithstanding the empty PID bug, there must be a misunderstanding on my part. My goal is to funnel all traffic from rootless containers on a podman network through a different gateway on the host's subnet, without affecting the host's own routing. I assumed that because pasta reflects the host's configuration into podman's network namespace, this new gateway would be visible from within, and it would be a matter of changing the namespace's default route. However, changes to the routing table were not reflected in my containers, and trying to assign a gateway through config resulted in the issue you see here.

Now that I think about it, there's no NAT between this namespace and the host through pasta, so I don't imagine custom routing could be done this way. How could I better approach this? I'm still learning.

> In particular what happens if you pass that one the cli
> 
> $ podman run --network pasta:--gateway,1.1.1.1 quay.io/libpod/testimage:20241011 true
> Error: pasta failed with exit code 1:
> Couldn't set IPv4 route(s) in guest: Network is unreachable
> 
> Trying with --network pasta:--gateway,1.1.1.1,--pid,/tmp/pasta.pid shows that it fails to startup and created an empty pid file and thus podman does indeed fail to read it.
> I guess we could handle this in podman to ignore empty pid file there.

Running this command gives me the same response you've copied here. Thanks all.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
